artifact.title: GLM-5.2 Neimeng Top-Five Kernel MFU Audit

request_contract.cache_policy: flush before warmup and again before measured request

runtime_contract.effective_backends: DSA attention; TRT-LLM DSA prefill; SGL-kernel DSA TopK; CUTLASS MoE; no MoE all-to-all
runtime_contract.cuda_graphs: decode and piecewise CUDA graphs disabled

profiler_contract.nsys_scope: CUDA/NVTX/OSRT; cudaProfilerApi range; CUDA graph node tracing; sampling and CPU context-switch tracing disabled
profiler_contract.ncu_scope: target all child processes; CUDA device 2; per-launch-config; one launch; kernel replay; cache-control all; clock-control none

device.identity_note: The cluster is named B300, while Nsys and nvidia-smi report NVIDIA L20D for CUDA device 2 and NCU reports CC 10.3 / architecture GB110. The 4,500-TFLOP/s denominator is retained only for like-for-like B300 normalization.

top_five.label: RopeQuantizeKernel

normalized_mfu.convention: Useful FP8-equivalent model FLOPs divided by kernel time and 4,500 TFLOP/s dense FP8 peak.

grouped_moe.ncu_scope: One representative gate/up launch. Gate/up and down share the same compiled name and launch geometry, so per-launch-config NCU selects the first instance.

deepgemm_dominant_name.ncu_scope: The selected NCU row is the first dominant compiled-name instance, q_b_proj. Dynamic problem sizes reuse the same compiled kernel name.

gather.reason: TopK gather/index movement is not dense Tensor-Core work. The attempted NCU counter report is invalid.

rope.reason: RoPE plus FP8 quantization is elementwise and data movement, not dense Tensor-Core work. Only launch geometry is retained.

audit.evidence: Four independent reports; every measured request completed
Request exit 0; NCU exit 0; report exported
Request exit 0; complete nonzero report; wrapper exit 9 followed intentional server shutdown
Kernel replay raised LaunchFailed; requests disconnected and exported performance counters were zero
Request completed but no report was emitted; range metrics would also aggregate the range rather than preserve per-kernel values
